core_mechanism: a critical-threshold (separatrix) structure governing collisions of localized nonlinear excitations, in which energy exchange with an internal degree of freedom produces sharp capture-versus-escape transitions whose boundary is a critical impact speed that depends on a second internal control parameter, yielding multiple threshold branches in the two-parameter control space
brief_reason: The abstract reports that the final state of kink collisions (scattering, capture, or long-lived bound states) switches sharply across two distinct phase-dependent critical-velocity branches, with the relative phase acting as an effective internal degree of freedom controlling the transition.
